denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 05/21/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W102835002          Group #: GRP-1358
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-153067
Claim #: CLM-2026-206975
Date of Service (requested): 05/01/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Ridgeway Physician Services

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 05/03/2026 for GLP-1 receptor agonist (semaglutide 2.4 mg) related to Obesity with BMI 42 and prediabetes (E66.01). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: GLP-1 receptor agonist (semaglutide 2.4 mg)
Diagnosis (as submitted): Obesity with BMI 42 and prediabetes (E66.01)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM138 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
The requested service is not medically necessary because it does not meet the plan's medical necessity criteria.
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM138 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not meet medical necessity criteria because the service is not medically necessary for this member at this time. The duration of GLP-1 receptor agonist (semaglutide 2.4 mg) exceeds plan limits without new clinical review. Continuation requires updated documentation not received.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director before initiating a formal appeal on the same authorization decision.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W102835002) and reference UM-2026-153067. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 19-year-old member has Obesity with BMI 42 and prediabetes (E66.01). She has been treated under commercial coverage with GLP-1 receptor agonist (semaglutide 2.4 mg) recommended as the next appropriate step. BMI 42; HbA1c 6.2%; failed structured lifestyle program 6 months. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that duration excessive bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: The brief matches the assigned UHC / endocrine cell and executes sub_tactic 'duration_excessive' with a clinically appropriate Obesity with BMI 42 and prediabetes (E66.01) → GLP-1 receptor agonist (semaglutide 2.4 mg) pair.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic UHC correspondence with UnitedHealthcare medical policy and procedural tone.
